data.topic: BYOD device data deletion and retention obligations

data.query: BYOD device data deletion and retention obligations
data.conclusion: Analyzing BYOD termination obligations and retention schedules across IT Security and Data Governance policies.

data.query: BYOD device data deletion and retention obligations

citations.passage: Upon employee termination or device transfer, all company data must be permanently and immediately deleted from personal devices. Verified deletion certificate required within 24 hours.
citations.topic: BYOD device data deletion and retention obligations

citations.passage: All employee communications, work products, and associated metadata must be retained for a minimum of 7 years for audit and legal compliance purposes.
citations.topic: BYOD device data deletion and retention obligations

data.topic: BYOD device data deletion and retention obligations

data.conclusion: Validation passed: both obligations trigger at the exact same moment (termination). Deletion makes retention impossible. Retention makes deletion impossible.

data.topic: BYOD device data deletion and retention obligations
data.conflict: BYOD termination: instant deletion vs 7-year retention

data.conclusion: Affects 450 active BYOD users. Every termination event triggers the contradiction. 12 planned terminations this quarter place the company in simultaneous violation of both policies.

data.category: Security Risk (undeleted company data on personal devices)
Governance Risk (audit trail gap on retention)
Operational Risk (impossible compliance on every termination)

citations.passage: Upon employee termination or device transfer, all company data must be permanently and immediately deleted from personal devices.
citations.topic: BYOD device data deletion and retention obligations

citations.passage: All employee communications, work products, and associated metadata must be retained for a minimum of 7 years.
citations.topic: BYOD device data deletion and retention obligations

data.conclusion: Every termination of a BYOD user creates a forced policy violation. The conflict is not theoretical — it fires for each of 450 active BYOD users. Risk is HIGH, not CRITICAL, because no regulatory deadline is imminent; however, any litigation discovery request will expose the gap.

risk_categories: Security Risk (undeleted company data on personal devices)
Governance Risk (audit trail gap on retention)
Operational Risk (impossible compliance on every termination)
assessment.reasoning: IT Security Policy §6.3 mandates immediate permanent deletion of company data from personal devices at termination. Data Governance Policy §11.2 requires 7-year retention of all employee work products. Both obligations activate at the same instant for the same data on the same device. Deletion prevents retention. Retention prevents deletion. There is no path to simultaneous compliance. 450 BYOD users are currently exposed.

supporting_evidence.passage: Upon employee termination or device transfer, all company data must be permanently and immediately deleted from personal devices.
supporting_evidence.topic: BYOD device data deletion and retention obligations

supporting_evidence.passage: All employee communications, work products, and associated metadata must be retained for a minimum of 7 years.
supporting_evidence.topic: BYOD device data deletion and retention obligations

potential_consequences: Terminated employees retain access pathways to undeleted company data on personal devices.
Litigation discovery requests will reveal 7-year retention gaps for terminated BYOD users.
12 planned terminations this quarter each create a forced compliance violation.
assessment.uncertainty: Specific financial penalties are not quantified without grounded regulatory evidence. Audit exposure depends on jurisdiction.

data.conclusion: Implement corporate MDM (Mobile Device Management) solution that separates company data into an encrypted container on personal devices. Container can be remotely wiped (satisfying IT Security §6.3) while the MDM platform retains a copy in corporate storage for 7 years (satisfying Data Governance §11.2). IT and Legal must jointly define the MDM policy within Q3 2026.

data.title: BYOD termination: instant deletion vs 7-year retention
data.affected: 450 active BYOD users. Every termination triggers a forced simultaneous violation of both policies.
data.deadline: Q3 2026 — align before next termination cycle
data.resolution: Implement MDM with encrypted corporate container on personal devices. Remote-wipe satisfies IT Security §6.3; MDM cloud copy satisfies Data Governance §11.2 7-year retention. IT and Legal co-own within Q3 2026.
